text:
There, nestled in the dense shadow of an ancient oak, was a forgotten garden. The roses slept amongst ivies, their petals wilting like ancient leaves. A solitary figure strolled along a path that traced a circle around a fountain shimmering with stars. This wanderer was a humble collector of peculiar flowers, their colors like faded rubies.

In the farthest corner grew a white blossom that couldn’t possibly belong. It glowed softly, a moonlit mystery. With reverence, our friend named it Luna. Every day she would return, drawing nearer until their blooms touched. She told Luna anecdotes of old, each petal a memory.

Time passed, and a new visitor arrived. A roguish fox with eyes like velvet. He saw Luna's devotion and decided to become her protector and companion. Together they plotted to save Luna. With cleverness and kindness they fought the encroaching darkness—plucking roses that glowed just as brightly as Luna.
